preamble: The diphthong /oi/ has undergone various transformations in the history of languages. In French, a notable shift involved its monophthongization to an open-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ/ in certain contexts, particularly evident in the evolution of verb conjugations.
phoneticEffects: Monophthongization, unrounding, fronting, and lowering of the diphthong's vocalic elements

examples.note: In Old French, 'oi' was pronounced as a diphthong, either /oi̯/ or /o̯e/ (or /wɛ/). In Modern French, in words like 'était', this has monophthongized to /ɛ/.

sources: Question about historical pronunciation : r/asklinguistics - Reddit.
F422 HISTORY OF THE FRENCH LANGUAGE prerequisite: FRENCH 221 Catalog Description: "A linguistic study of the phonological, - Western Kentucky University.
